# Triggr

> Triggr is a controller-first reaction speed game built for modern browsers using the Gamepad API.

## Overview

Triggr shows random Cross, Circle, Square, and Triangle prompts after a variable delay. Players press the
matching controller face button before the timeout. The app measures reaction time, grades successful hits,
tracks misses and streaks, and stores recent game summaries in memory.

## Features

- 3D controller scene
- Standard Gamepad API face-button mapping
- Keyboard fallback using X, O, S, and T
- Random 0.8-2.6 second prompt delay
- 2.5 second reaction timeout
- Fast, Good, and Slow reaction grades
- Streak celebrations and reaction sound cues
- Last 8 game summaries with top-3 ranking
- Dark and light themes
- Responsive desktop and mobile layout

## How To Use

1. Open https://triggr-reaction.vercel.app/
2. Connect a controller using USB or Bluetooth.
3. Focus the page and press any controller button once to activate the browser Gamepad API.
4. Choose the number of rounds and start the game.
5. Press the face button that matches each prompt.

## Links

- Website: https://triggr-reaction.vercel.app/
- Repository: https://github.com/joshtom/reaction-speed-game
